| [CQC: Regulation 15, Premises and equipment](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/regulations-service-providers-and-managers/health-social-care-act/regulation-15) | Clean, suitable, maintained premises and equipment. |
| [CQC: Regulation 16, Complaints](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-providers/regulations-enforcement/regulation-16-receiving-acting-complaints) | Accessible complaint system and CQC information requests. |
| [CQC: Regulation 17, Good governance](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/regulations-service-providers-and-managers/health-social-care-act/regulation-17) | Governance systems, audit, risk, information, records, staff and management records. |
| [CQC: Regulation 18, Staffing](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/regulations-service-providers-and-managers/health-social-care-act/regulation-18) | Sufficient qualified, competent, skilled, experienced staff and support. |
| [CQC: Regulation 19, Fit and proper persons employed](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/regulations-service-providers-and-managers/health-social-care-act/regulation-19) | Recruitment checks and fitness for clinical roles. |
| [CQC: Regulation 20, Duty of candour](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-providers/all-services/regulation-20-duty-candour) | Openness and notifiable safety incident duties for regulated providers. |
| [CQC: Assessment framework](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/assessment/assessment-framework) | Safe, effective, caring, responsive, and well-led assessment lens. |
| [CQC: Appendix 1, the 34 quality statements](https://www.cqc.org.uk/publications/review-cqcs-single-assessment-framework-and-its-implementation/appendix-1-34-quality-statements) | Provider quality statement names and mapping across the five key questions. |
| [CQC: Evidence categories](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/assessment/evidence-categories) | Six evidence categories used to assess quality statements. |
| [CQC: Governance, management and sustainability](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/assessment/single-assessment-framework/well-led/governance-management-sustainability) | Well-led quality statement and evidence themes. |
| [CQC: Medicines optimisation](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/providers/assessment/single-assessment-framework/safe/medicines-optimisation) | Medicines safety, best practice, prescribing/supply/administration, transfer information, and controlled-drugs oversight. |
| [CQC: Independent healthcare services](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-providers/independent-healthcare/independent-healthcare-services) | Definition and examples of independent healthcare providers. |
| [CQC: Local authority assessment framework](https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-regulation/local-authorities/assessment-framework) | Distinction between provider assessment and local authority assurance using four themes and nine quality statements. |
| [RFL: Medical Management](https://www.rugby-league.com/governance/medical/medical-management) | IMMOFP/iIMMOFP roles, professional registration, indemnity, and training requirements. |
| [RFL: Medical Standards 2026 PDF](https://www.rugby-league.com/uploads/docs/Medical%20Standards%202026.pdf) | Current RFL mandatory medical personnel, records, information sharing, EAP, equipment, anti-doping, concussion, cardiac screening, and medical policy controls. |
| [RFL: Concussion Statement](https://www.rugby-league.com/governance/medical/concussion/concussion-statement) | Professional game medical provision, concussion governance, CPD, community first-aider expectations. |
| [RFL: Anti-Doping](https://www.rugby-league.com/governance/rules-and-regulations/anti-doping-integrity-betting) | Rugby League anti-doping rules, TUEs, testing, and player/support-person obligations. |
| [UKAD: Medicine and TUE Hub](https://www.ukad.org.uk/medicine) | TUE concept and medicine checking route. |
| [UKAD: Search Check Apply](https://www.ukad.org.uk/searchcheckapply) | Medication checking, Global DRO, TUE Wizard, and athlete responsibility. |
| [UKAD: Managing Supplement Risks](https://www.ukad.org.uk/athletes/managing-supplement-risks) | Strict liability and supplement contamination risk. |
| [GMC: Good practice in prescribing and managing medicines and devices](https://www.gmc-uk.org/professional-standards/the-professional-standards/good-practice-in-prescribing-and-managing-medicines-and-devices) | Prescribing competence, safe assessment, records, controlled-drugs safeguards, follow-up, and adverse reaction reporting. |
| [GMC: Confidentiality, employment and similar purposes](https://www.gmc-uk.org/professional-standards/the-professional-standards/confidentiality---disclosing-information-for-employment-insurance-and-similar-purposes/disclosing-information-for-employment-insurance-and-similar-purposes) | Sports-team dual obligations, consent, relevance, reports, and disclosure boundaries. |
| [Fieldfisher: Sports organisations and CQC registration](https://www.fieldfisher.com/en/services/public-and-regulatory/public-regulatory-law-blog/sports-organisations-and-cqc-registration) | Secondary analysis on sports organisations, registration rarity, and governance burden. |
| [LLM Wiki](https://llm-wiki.org/) | Reference pattern for a Material for MkDocs-based LLM-facing wiki. |
| [mkdocs-llms-source on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/mkdocs-llms-source/) | LLM outputs: `/llms.txt`, `/llms-full.txt`, and per-page Markdown copies. |
| [Material for MkDocs](https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material/) | Documentation theme and site structure. |
